Senior Platform Engineer

Engineering – Developer Platform Remote · United States Full time

We are looking for a Senior Platform Engineer to shape the developer experience for engineers across ExampleCo. You will build internal tools, golden paths, and self-service infrastructure that let product teams ship faster and safer. This role reports to the Head of Platform and partners closely with SRE and Security.

What you will do

  • Design and build the internal developer platform: service templates, CI/CD pipelines, deployment tooling, and runtime primitives.
  • Own one or more platform capabilities end-to-end — from roadmap and RFC to rollout, adoption metrics, and on-call.
  • Collaborate with product teams to identify toil and translate pain points into platform investments.
  • Write clear documentation, runbooks, and internal blog posts that raise the engineering bar across the org.
  • Participate in the platform on-call rotation and help establish SLOs for internal services.

What we are looking for

  • 5+ years of backend or platform engineering experience, with meaningful contributions to internal developer tooling.
  • Hands-on experience with Kubernetes, Terraform, GitHub Actions or similar CI systems, and one of Go/Python/TypeScript.
  • Product sense for developer experience — you think about your users (engineers) and measure adoption.
  • Comfort writing and reviewing design docs; clear communicator in async-first teams.

Bonus points for

  • Experience with service mesh, OpenTelemetry, or policy-as-code (OPA).
  • Contributions to open-source platform tooling.
  • Background supporting multi-tenant production environments at scale.

Compensation and benefits

The target salary range for this role is $180,000 – $230,000 base plus equity. ExampleCo offers comprehensive medical, dental, vision, 401k match, and an annual learning stipend.

About ExampleCo

ExampleCo builds developer-facing APIs used by thousands of companies worldwide. We are a distributed team of 120 people across North America and Europe.
